enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request to share his thoughts on Elliot Rodger and the 2014 Isla Vista/UC Santa Barbara shooting. He breaks down Rodger's psychology as a 'perfect storm' of Asperger's syndrome, sociopathy, wealth-driven entitlement, parental neglect, and his father's nihilistic worldview. Kyle also riffs on gender differences in mass violence, religion's social utility, and other cases of wealthy people committing crimes when they feel their entitlement was violated.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: a perfect storm of mental illness, wealth-driven entitlement, parental neglect, isolation, and absorbing his father's nihilistic worldview
overlaps with sociopathy in that both involve inability to read others' emotions and social cues
his atheism and cynical worldview likely contributed to Rodger's hopeless, nihilistic thinking
extreme wealth breeds entitlement, and when that entitlement is frustrated it can lead to violent behavior
men escalate to open violence while women express anger through social or indirect means like poison
religion serves a useful function by providing a moral framework that teaches right from wrong
most people are capable of killing under extreme enough circumstances; it is a sliding scale
his behavior was 'typically LA' in that he seemed to imagine his killing spree as a path to celebrity and a movie being made about him

entities: Sheldon Cooper (Big Bang Theory character, cited as Asperger's example)
Peg Entwistle (actress who jumped off the Hollywood sign, referenced vaguely)
neighbor of Kyle's wife's mother (convicted of murder)
